authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (McKinney Lake not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (McKinney Lake not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50(b)(152) — Tahoe Lake and tributaries (the tributary closure applies to streams 'upstream to the first lake', not to the lakes themselves)
sources.quotedText: (152) Tahoe Lake and tributaries (Placer and El Dorado Cos.). (A) Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout (B) Tahoe Lake within 300 feet of the mouth of its tributaries. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Lake layer record for gaz_id 263396, "McKinney Lake", Lake, Placer County, CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":263396,"gaz_name":"McKinney Lake","gaz_featureclass":"Lake","fcode":39004,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Placer"}; geometry (Web Mercator -13378072.309, 4727503.613) converts to lon -120.177268, lat 39.040703, matching the file's recorded coordinate (-120.17718, 39.04075) to within ~10m.

parameters.note: McKinney Lake is a small lake in the McKinney Creek drainage west of Lake Tahoe (Placer Co., in the McKinney-Rubicon backcountry). It is not listed by name in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake regulation §5.85(a)(1) (open all year, five-trout daily / ten-trout possession). Note: the Lake Tahoe tributary closure §7.50(b)(152)(A) governs Tahoe tributary streams only 'upstream to the first lake' and does not extend to lakes on the drainage such as McKinney Lake, which follow the general lake regulation.

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water, and the Lake Tahoe tributary closure applies only to streams upstream to the first lake, not to lakes)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license. McKinney Lake is wholly within California (Placer Co.); no Nevada reciprocity applies.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
